hydross.SCORECARD_FINDING: 46th percentile, the strongest relative kill of the raid so far; overheal (57.7%) sat just under the sample's worst parse (58.4%), and HPM (2.86) ran at 0.54x the sample average. Active time (97.9%) was in line, and no deaths this kill.
hydross.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Flash Heal (10.6%) undershot the sample's 32.6% share, the largest gap this kill; Prayer of Mending (34.3%) and Renew (22.1%) both ran well ahead of the sample instead.
hydross.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Mana Potion went unused against a 59% sample usage rate. Power Word: Shield also shows 0 casts here, but the Top 100 sample itself sits near 0% usage on this ability across most bosses - a known ranking-metric bias in the benchmark population, not a real norm to read Lasmur's own usage against.
hydross.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (93.1%) ran well above the sample average (71.0%) - nearly all healing landed on the top 5 recipients - while the top target (Thugalina, 32.3%) took a share close to the sample's typical concentration (37.3%).

lurker.SCORECARD_FINDING: The lowest percentile of the raid (12th); HPS (585) ran at just 0.25x the sample average, the widest HPS gap of any kill so far, and overheal (43.3%) edged past the sample's worst parse (40.5%) by 3 points. Active time (96.0%) stayed in line.
lurker.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Circle of Healing (33.1%) undershot the sample's dominant 71.2% share by the widest margin of the raid to this point.
lurker.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Shadowfiend, Chakra, and Mana Potion were all cast once, self-targeted and in line with the sample. No deaths this kill.
lurker.TARGET_FINDING: The most tank-concentrated kill of the raid so far: Captinhelp alone took 48.0% of healing, more than four times the sample's typical top-target share (11.9%), and coverage (82.2%) ran well above the sample average (45.7%).

leotheras.SCORECARD_FINDING: 35th percentile; HPS (790) ran at 0.28x the sample average, among the lowest ratios of the raid, though overheal (39.3%) barely crossed the sample's worst parse (38.4%). By far the deadliest kill of the entire raid - 22 deaths, more than double the next-highest kill (Solarian, 9) - and Lasmur died personally 3 times during it.
leotheras.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Circle of Healing (30.3%) undershot the sample's 77.3% share by the widest margin of any kill this raid; Prayer of Mending (27.3%) and Renew (21.0%) both ran well ahead of the sample to compensate.
leotheras.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Shadowfiend went unused against an 82% sample usage rate - a real gap, though Shadowfiend restores mana rather than preventing deaths. Blessing of Life was cast twice, both times on self. With 22 deaths clustered around this kill (including Lasmur's own 3), the nearest-cooldown timestamps land close to those two Blessing of Life casts, but Blessing of Life is a self-targeted buff, not a way to save another player - the death count here reads as a real, difficult pull for the whole raid rather than something a cooldown choice could have addressed.
leotheras.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (48.8%) and top-target concentration (12.3%, Captinhelp) both landed in line with the sample - target spread wasn't a factor on this kill; the death count above is the real story.

karathress.SCORECARD_FINDING: 64th percentile, the best relative kill of the raid so far; HPM (5.11, 0.65x the sample average) was the most efficient ratio of the raid to this point, and overheal (39.4%) sat just under the sample's worst parse (43.0%). This was also the one kill with no elixir active at all (per the gear audit below).
karathress.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Circle of Healing (24.9%) undershot the sample's 63.5% share; Renew (25.4%) and Prayer of Mending (21.5%) both ran well ahead of the sample instead.
karathress.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Shadowfiend, Chakra, and Mana Potion (2 casts) all landed in line with the sample. Two deaths this kill (Ghostpepperx, Megatauro); the nearest cooldowns (Mana Potion, Shadowfiend) are self-targeted resource tools, not saves, so neither reads as a real missed opportunity.
karathress.TARGET_FINDING: Braila took the largest healing share (40.3%), well above the sample's typical top-target concentration (16.4%) - a real, tank-heavy kill.

morogrim.SCORECARD_FINDING: 21st percentile; overheal (54.8%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (45.4%) by 9 points, and HPM (4.02) ran at 0.55x the sample average. The only two deaths this kill were both Lasmur's own, 15 seconds apart.
morogrim.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Circle of Healing (37.3%) undershot the sample's 58.4% share; Prayer of Mending (23.1%) ran well ahead of the sample's 8.4%.
morogrim.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Shadowfiend, Chakra, and Mana Potion all landed in line with the sample. Blessing of Life was cast twice, both on self. Neither of Lasmur's own two deaths this kill lines up with a real save opportunity - the nearest cooldowns (Mana Potion before the first, Blessing of Life after the second) are self-targeted resource/buff tools, not defensive cooldowns aimed at preventing death.
morogrim.TARGET_FINDING: The most tank-concentrated kill of the raid: Captinhelp took 53.2% of healing, more than triple the sample's typical top-target share (17.0%), with coverage (71.9%) also well above the sample average (44.0%).

vashj.SCORECARD_FINDING: 31st percentile; HPM (4.19) ran at 0.66x the sample average, and overheal (51.8%) sat comfortably below the sample's own worst parse (74.6%, unusually high for this boss). Active time, target coverage, and top-target concentration all landed in line with the sample.
vashj.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Prayer of Mending (22.6%) ran well ahead of the sample's 8.6% share - the largest gap this kill, and one of the few this raid running in the opposite direction from the usual Circle of Healing shortfall (26.6% here, close to the sample's 29.8%).
vashj.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Shadowfiend, Chakra, and Mana Potion all landed in line with the sample. Two deaths this kill (Ghostpepperx, Vajomee); the nearest cooldowns (Chakra, Mana Potion) are self-targeted, not saves, so neither reads as a real correlation.
vashj.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (48.6%) and top-target concentration (22.8%, Captinhelp) both landed in line with the sample.

alar.SCORECARD_FINDING: 30th percentile; overheal (53.6%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (46.7%) by 7 points, and HPM (3.42) ran at 0.58x the sample average. Active time (95.5%) was in line, and one death this kill.
alar.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Circle of Healing (14.1%) undershot the sample's 42.6% share; Renew (34.6%) and Power Word: Shield (19.6%) both ran well ahead of the sample to compensate.
alar.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Shadowfiend, Chakra, and Mana Potion all landed in line with the sample. The one death this kill (Captinhelp) trails the nearest Chakra cast by 28 seconds, too far to read as related.
alar.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (87.6%) ran well above the sample average (49.8%) - the widest coverage gap of the raid alongside Lurker - and the top target (Thugalina, 38.5%) also took a larger share than the sample's typical concentration (18.9%).

voidreaver.SCORECARD_FINDING: 47th percentile; HPS (2289, 0.61x the sample average) and HPM (8.84, 0.73x the sample average) were both the closest-to-sample-normal ratios of the raid, and overheal (26.6%) was the lowest absolute overheal of any kill this raid even though it still edged past this boss's tight worst-parse ceiling (23.5%) by 3 points. No deaths this kill.
voidreaver.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Circle of Healing (70.7%) was the dominant spell here, close to the sample's own 81.9% share - the smallest spell-composition gap of the raid.
voidreaver.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Shadowfiend, Chakra, and Mana Potion all landed in line with the sample. No deaths this kill.
voidreaver.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (48.2%) and top-target concentration (19.5%, Captinhelp) both ran somewhat above the sample average, consistent with the raid-wide pattern of healing landing on fewer targets than the Top 100 sample typically spreads across.

solarian.SCORECARD_FINDING: 22nd percentile; overheal (65.3%) was the highest absolute overheal of any kill this raid, exceeding the sample's worst parse (62.2%) by 3 points. Nine deaths landed within a roughly 12-second window mid-fight - a real, sudden burst rather than deaths spread across the pull.
solarian.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Flash Heal (15.0%) undershot the sample's 42.1% share, the largest gap this kill; Circle of Healing (46.6%) ran close to the sample's own 40.7%.
solarian.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Fear Ward was cast once, on Captinhelp - a real use of an ability only 8% of the Top 100 sample used at all this kill. The nine deaths cluster tightly around a Blessing of Life cast and a Chakra cast, both self-targeted utility rather than saves for other players, so the timing reads as a real wipe-adjacent burst rather than a missed defensive-cooldown opportunity.
solarian.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (53.6%) and top-target concentration (24.6%, Captinhelp) both landed in line with the sample - the death burst above is the real story on this kill, not target spread.

kaelthas.SCORECARD_FINDING: The strongest kill of the raid by percentile (71st); HPM (4.28, 0.73x the sample average) tied for the most efficient ratio of the raid, and active time (95.5%) ran above the sample average for the first time this raid. Overheal (49.7%) sat just under the sample's worst parse (52.3%).
kaelthas.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Circle of Healing (31.0%) undershot the sample's 44.6% share; Renew (22.0%) and Prayer of Mending (22.8%) both ran ahead of the sample to compensate, the same pattern as most of the raid.
kaelthas.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Shadowfiend, Chakra, Mana Potion (2 casts), and Fear Ward (on Captinhelp, in line with an 87% sample usage rate here) all landed in line with the sample. Four deaths this kill; the nearest cooldowns (Blessing of Life, Chakra) are self-targeted and trail the deaths by 20-107 seconds, too far and the wrong kind of cooldown to read as related.
kaelthas.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (63.8%) ran above the sample average (42.5%); top-target concentration (21.8%, Captinhelp) landed in line.

RaidOverview.GEAR_CONSISTENCY_FINDING: not fully consistent - Finger1 differs across kills and is flagged for review, alongside one benign single-kill Main Hand swap
RaidOverview.GEAR_FINDING_NOTE: The Finger1 variant doesn't read as a clean single-kill swap - worth confirming whether it was a deliberate ring change or a real equipping gap on some kills. All 9 enchantable slots carry a permanent enchant, and consumables were complete on 9 of 10 kills; Fathom-Lord Karathress is the one exception, with no elixir of either kind active that pull.
RaidOverview.RAID_SUMMARY_FINDING: Two consistent, raid-wide patterns stand out. First, Blessing of Life - a self-cast the Top 100 sample uses on only 3-8% of parses per boss - was cast on 8 of 10 kills, the most consistent cooldown deviation of the raid. Second, Circle of Healing undershot the Top 100 sample's share on 8 of 10 kills, with Renew, Prayer of Mending, and Power Word: Shield making up the difference; Power Word: Shield's own near-0% Top 100 usage rate on most bosses is a known benchmark bias, not a real norm, so its low usage isn't read as underuse anywhere in this report. Leotheras the Blind was by far the deadliest kill of the raid (22 deaths, more than double the next-highest kill), and Lasmur died personally 3 times during it, plus 2 more times on Morogrim Tidewalker - the only two kills flagged for self-deaths this raid. Solarian's 9 deaths landed in a tight ~12-second burst rather than spread across the pull. The three weakest kills by percentile were Lurker (12th), Morogrim (21st), and Solarian (22nd); Kael'thas Sunstrider was the strongest (71st).
RaidOverview.RAID_WARNING_BANNER: Leotheras the Blind was by far the deadliest kill of the raid (22 deaths, more than double the next-highest kill), and Lasmur died personally 3 times during it - plus 2 more times on Morogrim Tidewalker, the only other kill with a self-death this raid.
